Identity.History: Khosrow I ruled the Sasanian Empire from 531–579 AD and became one of its greatest monarchs, remembered as Anushirvan, “of the immortal soul.” He strengthened royal authority, reformed taxation and the army, and reduced the power of overmighty nobles. His reign saw major wars with Byzantium, campaigns in Arabia and the Caucasus, and the destruction of the Hephthalite Empire with Turkic aid. A patron of learning and philosophy, he made Ctesiphon a center of culture and statecraft. His rule marked a high point of Sasanian power.
